questions.question: Explain the primary function of a sensor in an IoT endpoint and provide two examples.
keyPoints: Primary function: detect and measure physical properties and convert to electrical signals
Example 1: Temperature sensor (thermistor) for thermal conditions
Example 2: Motion sensor (PIR) for detecting movement

questions.question: Compare MCU and SoC in IoT devices and discuss key factors for choosing between them.
keyPoints: MCU: Single chip with processor, memory, and I/O for simple control tasks
SoC: Integrates modem, GPU, and more components for complex applications
Key factors: processing power, integrated connectivity, power budget, cost, application complexity

questions.question: What makes IoT endpoints 'constrained devices' and why is this important?
keyPoints: Limited processing power (CPU), memory (RAM/Flash), and energy availability
Deployed in remote locations requiring battery operation for extended periods
Drives need for efficient hardware design and lightweight protocols

questions.question: Describe the complete signal chain within a typical IoT sensor endpoint from physical phenomenon to network transmission.
keyPoints: Transducer/sensor detects physical phenomenon and creates analog signal
Signal conditioner amplifies and filters the analog signal
ADC converts analog to digital values for the microcontroller
MCU processes data and formats it into network packets
Communication module transmits the packet to gateway or cloud
